## [00:00 - 03:07]

Okay, so now we're at the stage where we're going to get our audiences ready for our rapid fire test. That's the first stage of rapid fire testing is we want to see which cold audiences are going to be the most responsive to our offer. And then once we identify that, then we go into the creative testing or we can even go into the offer testing where we test out the offer. So here what we're going to do is just refer back to the funnel that we scoped out and you can see that there's really two types of audiences that we're going to test inside of this rapid fire segment. And we're going to try to run interest-based audience as well as a look-alike. Now it's important that you establish a budget for this as well so that I'm going to just write out 300 so that you know how many audiences you can create. You want to be spending at least $15 per ad set, meaning you really, you can't get too granular if you have a $300 budget for example. However, if you've onboarded your client under the expectation that the first month is testing and that they should dedicate between $1,000 to $2,000 during this testing phase, it could be that your first round of rapid fire testing you have $1,000 to play with. And so you can really segment out a lot of different interests, maybe even countries, and you could potentially even segment out by placement at this level. With a smaller ad spend like $300, we're basically going to be fairly restricted with how many ad sets we can create. The thing here is that you're going to have to have at least 100 data points for that country in order to be able to create that lookalike. So just be aware of that. And it's, there we go. And it's a good idea to build your lookalikes off the highest caliber of person that comes through your funnel. And those are people that purchase, right? That's ultimately what you're after, customers. If you don't have that, just take it a step back. Do they have website visitors? Are there people watching their videos?
